briefdescription.para: Configure operation of a eSPI controller.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to override eSPI controller capabilities.
If this eSPI controller is acting as slave, the values set here will be discovered as part through the GET_CONFIGURATION command issued by the eSPI master during initialization.
If this eSPI controller is acting as master, the values set here will be used by eSPI master to determine minimum common capabilities with eSPI slave then send via SET_CONFIGURATION command.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
the device runtime configuration for the eSPI controller.
If successful.
General input / output error, failed to configure device.
invalid capabilities, failed to configure device.
capability not supported by eSPI slave.

briefdescription.para: Set one or more SAF protection regions.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides an interface to override the default flash protection regions of the SAF controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the SAF protection region structure.
If successful.
General input / output error, failed to configure device.
invalid capabilities, failed to configure device.
capability not supported by eSPI slave.

detaileddescription.para: This routine activates the SAF block and should only be called after SAF has been configured and the eSPI Master has enabled the Flash Channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If successful
if failed to activate SAF.

briefdescription.para: Query to see if SAF is ready.

detaileddescription.para: This routine allows to check if SAF is ready before use.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If eSPI SAF is ready.
otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sends a read request packet for slave attached flash.

detaileddescription.para: This routines provides an interface to send a request to read the flash component shared between the eSPI master and eSPI slaves.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the representation of read flash transaction.
eSPI flash logical channel transactions not supported.
eSPI flash channel is not ready or disabled by master.
General input / output error, failed request to master.

briefdescription.para: Sends a write request packet for slave attached flash.

detaileddescription.para: This routines provides an interface to send a request to write to the flash components shared between the eSPI master and eSPI slaves.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the representation of write flash transaction.
eSPI flash logical channel transactions not supported.
eSPI flash channel is not ready or disabled by master.
General input / output error, failed request to master.

briefdescription.para: Sends a write request packet for slave attached flash.

detaileddescription.para: This routines provides an interface to send a request to write to the flash components shared between the eSPI master and eSPI slaves.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the representation of erase flash transaction.
eSPI flash logical channel transactions not supported.
eSPI flash channel is not ready or disabled by master.
General input / output error, failed request to master.

briefdescription.para: Response unsuccessful completion for slave attached flash.

detaileddescription.para: This routines provides an interface to response that transaction is invalid and return unsuccessful completion from target to controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the representation of flash transaction.
eSPI flash logical channel transactions not supported.
eSPI flash channel is not ready or disabled by master.
General input / output error, failed request to master.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct espi_callback *callback, espi_callback_handler_t handler, enum espi_bus_event evt_type)
memberdef.name: espi_saf_init_callback

detaileddescription.para: Helper to initialize a struct espi_callback properly.

parameterdescription.para: A valid Application's callback structure pointer.
A valid handler function pointer.
indicates the eSPI event relevant for the handler. for VWIRE_RECEIVED event the data will indicate the new level asserted

detaileddescription.para:
dev
Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
callback
A valid Application's callback structure pointer.
0 if successful, negative errno code on failure.
Callbacks may be added to the device from within a callback handler invocation, but whether they are invoked for the current eSPI event is not specified.
Note: enables to add as many callback as needed on the same device.

dev
Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
callback
A valid application's callback structure pointer.
0 if successful, negative errno code on failure.
It is explicitly permitted, within a callback handler, to remove the registration for the callback that is running, i.e. callback. Attempts to remove other registrations on the same device may result in undefined behavior, including failure to invoke callbacks that remain registered and unintended invocation of removed callbacks.
Note: enables to remove as many callbacks as added through espi_add_callback().

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for eSPI SAF (Serial Attached Flash) controllers.
